nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname жЁӮ, meaning вҖҳhappinessвҖҷ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (жЁӮзҲ¶), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799вҖ“766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (жЁӮиҺ’) inherited Le (жЁӮ) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname еӢ’: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (з–ҸеӢ’), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC вҖ“25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC вҖ“220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265вҖ“420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname еІі (or е¶Ҫ), meaning вҖҳhigh mountainвҖҷ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (еӣӣеІі or еӣӣе¶Ҫ), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname жЁӮ, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames йҷё, й№ҝ, йҖҜ and зҰ„, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname жқҺ, meaning вҖҳplumвҖҷ: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname зҗҶ (also pronounced Li; meaning вҖҳrulesвҖҷ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (еҲ©иІһ), son of an official called Li Zheng (зҗҶеҫө) in the late Shang dynasty (1600вҖ“1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (жқҺ вҖҳplumвҖҷ), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, зҗҶ, is said to be from Li Guan (зҗҶе®ҳ), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (жқҺиҖі), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (еҸұжқҺ) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386вҖ“534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618вҖ“907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (жқҺ), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname й»Һ, meaning вҖҳblackвҖҷ or вҖҳmultitudeвҖҷ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (й»Һ), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600вҖ“1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046вҖ“771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (й»ҺдҫҜ, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (зҙ й»Һ) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386вҖ“534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Wake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

occupational name for вҖҳ(man of) the watchвҖҷ, from Middle English wak, wake вҖҳwake, vigil, watch, period of guard dutyвҖҷ (Old English wacu, Old Norse vaka). It alternates with the synonymous Middle English wache (Old English wacce), hence the variant Watch. The surname was also interchangeable with Waite (the equivalent Old French term) which, like Wake, was sometimes translated as Latin vigil вҖҳ(one who is) watchful, alertвҖҷ. Compare the synonymous Vail . When the bearers had high social status, the surname probably denoted an officer with responsibility for the security of a castle, town, or royal person. Compare Wakeman .
variant of   Wack  .

variants.name: Vail
meanings: English: occupational name from Old French de la veille вҖҳof the watch, watchmanвҖҷ, with loss of de; compare Wake . There has been some confusion with the topographic name Vale and the nickname Veal .
Scottish: shortened and altered form of McPhail , Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac PhГ il вҖҳson of PaulвҖҷ.
Irish: variant of   Veale  .

variants.name: Waite
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name for a watchman, either in a town or castle, from Anglo-Norman French waite вҖҳwatchman, member of the watchвҖҷ (of ancient Germanic origin; compare Wachter ). In the Middle Ages the town waits typically combined the functions of watchmen and musicians through being trumpeters or pipers. There may also have been some late confusion with White or Wheat .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names В© Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek MargarД«tД“s, from margaron вҖҳpearlвҖҷ, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean вҖҳpearlвҖҷ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430вҖ“82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489вҖ“1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek MargarД«tД“s. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek MargarД«tД“s, from margaron вҖҳpearlвҖҷ, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean вҖҳpearlвҖҷ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430вҖ“82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489вҖ“1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Le Wake was born about 1165 as the daughter of Baldwin Wake. She had at least 1 daughter with Sir Robert de Vere Lord of Twywell. She died in 1206, at the age of 42.
